name: Pinwheels / Ambient Fixtures
organization: MIT Media Lab Tangible Media Group
short_description: Computer-controlled pinwheels and a Water Lamp that spin and ripple in a "bit wind," displaying digital data streams as ambient movement at the periphery of attention.
long_description: Pinwheels are kinetic ambient displays from MIT's Tangible Media Group: arrays of motorized pinwheels (and a companion Water Lamp projecting computational water ripples onto a ceiling) whose spinning speed is driven by online data such as stock activity, web traffic, or network packets. Part of the ambientROOM / Ambient Fixtures vision, they treat architectural space itself as an interface, presenting information through subtle background movement and sound rather than foreground screens. The work matters to physical AI and interaction design as a foundational example of calm, ambient, tangible representation of digital information in physical form—shaping how later systems convey state through embodied motion instead of explicit displays.
ai_note: The system is a mechanical kinetic display: computer-controlled motors map data-stream values to pinwheel spin speed, with no machine learning or AI inference involved.

images.caption: Array of computer-controlled pinwheels from MIT Tangible Media Group's Pinwheels ambient display project.
images.alt_text: Multiple small pinwheels mounted in an architectural space, spinning to display digital information as ambient movement.

sources.title: Pinwheels
sources.publisher: MIT Media Lab Tangible Media Group

sources.title: Water Lamp and Pinwheels: Ambient Projection of Digital Information into Architectural Space
Pinwheels: Visualizing Information Flow in an Architectural Space

interactions.title: Watch the network breathe as a 'bit wind'
interactions.description: An array of pinwheels is driven by a live data stream so they spin faster or slower as the value changes; a busy stretch of network or server packet activity reads as a quickening whir of blades at the edge of the room.

interactions.title: Feel the markets without a screen
interactions.description: The pinwheels are mapped to data such as stock-market activity or website hits, so a person sitting nearby senses a rising or falling trend as a change in spin speed rather than by checking a monitor.

interactions.title: Rain of bits rippling on the ceiling
interactions.description: In the companion Water Lamp, a light shines up through a pan of water while computer-controlled solenoids tap the surface in time with incoming digital data, casting shifting ripple shadows across the ceiling overhead.

interactions.title: Information you process at the edge of attention
interactions.description: The fixtures present digital activity as subtle background motion, light, and sound so a person can stay aware of an ongoing data stream peripherally, glancing over only when the movement shifts, instead of foregrounding a display.

interactions.title: Turn the whole room into the interface
interactions.description: Built as part of the ambientROOM, the pinwheels and Water Lamp let architectural space itself carry information through airflow and water movement, so the walls and ceiling of a study become the readout rather than a desktop.

interactions.title: A loved one's presence felt as gentle motion
interactions.description: Tie the spin to a remote partner's activity at their desk, so a slow idle turning quickens when they sit down to work, giving an unobtrusive sense of their presence in the room without a notification.

interactions.title: Ambient build-status for a workshop
interactions.description: Map the pinwheels to a continuous-integration pipeline so the array slows and stalls when a build breaks and spins smoothly when everything is green, letting a team read the health of their system from across the studio.
